rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A VS. JUSTIN HUNT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 17, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The claim of exemption is granted in part. The levying officer shall garnish $125 from each future pay bimonthly pay period until the judgment is paid. The levying officer shall release $3838.80 held to the judgment creditor. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: GREEN MATRIX SECURITIES LLC ASSIGNEE VS. JENNIFER CHEUNG JEW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order That Matters Specified In The Requests For Admissions Set Two To Joanna Cheung Aka Lampo Joanna Chueng Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for November 17, 2025, line 4. PLAINTIFF GREEN MATRIX SECURITIES LLC ASSIGNEE'S Motion For Order That Matters Specified In The Requests For Admissions Set Two To Joanna Cheung Aka Lampo Joanna Chueng Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions. The unopposed motion for a deemed admitted order is granted. Plaintiff has filed a proof of service of summons. Defendant Lampo Joanna Cheung aka Joanna Cheung has not responded to the Requests for Admission at issue. The matters in RFAs 10-17 (attached as Exhibit 1 to the Graves Declaration) are deemed admitted by this defendant. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Cheung is ordered to pay $50 to Plaintiff in sanctions within 30 days of entry of this order. (Id. [monetary sanctions are mandatory when a party does not respond to RFAs].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
